body:
The rules are very clear: "An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify."

I don't understand, then, why Gustavo Petro is leading in the polls, if his constitutional departure on August 7th doesn't apply, and the possibility of a coup, resignation, or removal is practically impossible.
It meant that old leader is not yet out of power until a new leader officially takes office through elections, similar to the Orban incident.
But if Petro leaves power constitutionally on August 7, this prediction will end in "No," because he did it constitutionally; it was not a dismissal.
Did you get the answer for this?

I think you're misreading that. I think what they mean is that the fact that his leaving is scheduled doesn't count until he actually leaves. If anyone else leaves before August 7, Petro will resolve to No even though his leaving was scheduled earlier.
Petro’s term is ending in May anyway. so according to the market rules, he will count as ‘no’. makes sense ?

body: Are you guys crazy? Check the rule!

as i understand there is shoulde no one,because Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.
I smelled the scam here, just sold the no, fuck the market

We're betting that Starmer will be out in July, ahead of Petro (Columbia). Really the question is will anyone challenge Burnham, if not Starmer will be first out. If there is a successful challenger, then Petro will probably be out sooner.
When Starmer leaves office he will have permanently left that Cabinet of UK government. It would be impossible for him to become the head of that House of Commons again.

"An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify."

Imo the most reasonable interpretation of this is "... or [an announcement of] a scheduled departure"

Under this interpretation the question is about when they actually physically leave office, regardless of how or why they leave.

I don't get the rules

What other way can a leader be out, if not resignation, or impeached?
The rules are very unclear here
None of us understand

Resolves according to the first person to cease to occupy their listed office. Pending removals or resignations do not qualify (e.g. an announcement of a future resignation, an election loss that leads to a scheduled transition of power, a temporary suspension, etc.)
resignation, impeachment, losing an election, dying
Milei will be out… private government info leaked by him… [link removed]
false
Petro will be out on August 7th. Starmer if he resigns would probably stay until the labour party conference in the last week of September.
Absolutely no way Starmer is there until the last week of September.

The observer:

The expectation among Starmer’s supporters is that he will set out a plan to stand down in September allowing his successor to be formally adopted at the Labour Party conference. “There are obviously downsides but we are where we are,” one senior figure said. “Keir has realised the game is up and it’s got to be a graceful exit. What he rightly wants to avoid is humiliation but the worst humiliation for Keir personally would be if he stands in a leadership election and is heavily beaten.
People already hate him after 6 months but gonna keep him on for another 3 just because? I have great doubts. Just be done with it.

People might dislike him but this is how politics operate. Nobody wants instability. There is no need for a general election, so he will resign this week and there will be a plan for him stepping down and new labour leadership will be appointed in September.

I basically agree with you but 3 months is just too long of a stretch. Having a lame duck PM is also a kind of instability and having Burnham just sitting around all summer would be awkward and he loses all the momentum.
For the market to resolve in yes for Petro only 7 weeks are needed. In the recent examples of Tony Blair, Theresa May and Boris Johnson from announcement to departure this period has been exceeded.
Well in any case clearly I'm an idiot for buying too high. We'll just have to see.
"one senior figure" I would just add that this seems to be no more than the speculation of perhaps as few as one unnamed source. But sure, I guess it's possible.

Starmer is underpriced here at 30%. Petro is the only realistic alternative and his earliest out date is August 7th. I'm linking the individual Starmer market where July 31st is at 62% and still rising. I'm just calling balls and strikes.

body: RULES: "An announcement of a resignation [...] will not alone qualify."
Does a normal end-of-term handover to an elected successor count (e.g. Petro on Aug 7), or does the "scheduled departure due to an election" clause mean only early/irregular removals qualify?
The important line is the first one: ” This market will resolve according to the first listed individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.”
It does not matter what causes the handover / removal.
lmao who's keep buying
Does death count??
Of course
YES

If Starmer runs unopposed, which is most likely given Wes Streeting endorsed him. Only 2 other front runners are Angela Rayner and Al Carns, which seems unlikely. I don't think Starmer ate a poison pill to see it drag on to September.
